title: Data Visualization
use_when: Create effective data visualizations with Python using matplotlib, seaborn, and plotly. Use when choosing chart types, building charts, creating publication-quality figures, or applying visualization design principles like accessibility and color theory.
do_not_use_when: Do not use when the user asks whether a visual, chart, dashboard, slide, or canvas may bias a decision, create overconfidence, hide alternatives, or distort stakeholder judgment; use visual-decision-making-review.
output_artifact: Data Visualization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Best when the user needs to create or improve a visualization artifact. Route decision-distortion questions to visual-decision-making-review.
failure_modes: Produces Data Visualization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Data Visualization as generic Analytics advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Data Visualization when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $data-visualization to turn this data context into a decision-ready Data Visualization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Data Visualization UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
